title: salix humilis var. microphylla
intro: Salix humilis var. microphylla, also known as the dwarf or prairie willow, is a shrub or small tree belonging to the willow family (Salicaceae) and is native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent qualities. The plant's health benefits include treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as being used to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Salix humilis var. microphylla can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote wound healing and soothe irritated skin.
